subject: LCARS 47988 widget gap analysis — 8 new components for dashboard

body.context: Session 91 cross-referenced the Classic LCARS 47988 design language (our canonical style) against the current dashboard implementation. Analysis used 4 reference images: Balsavor U.S.S. Centurion science screen, themes-2025 comparison sheet (6 variants), holodeck programming panel (CmdrKerner), and a home automation LCARS implementation. All references compared against docs/lcars-style-guide.md and docs/dashboard-ux-redesign.md.
body.style_reference: Classic LCARS 47988 (TNG, Michael Okuda). Our style guide already captures the color system correctly (--c-transport #ff9900, --c-knowledge #cc99cc, --c-health #9999ff, etc). The gaps concern component types, not colors.

missing_widgets.name: Numeric Data Grid
missing_widgets.lcars_source: Balsavor science screen — core display pattern. Numbers in alternating-color fixed-width capsules. Position encodes meaning, no labels needed.
missing_widgets.mesh_mapping: Transport message counts per session, trigger fire counts (20 triggers), claim verification rates, session velocity metrics.
missing_widgets.priority: HIGH — this represents the signature LCARS data display pattern. Without it, the dashboard reads as a web app wearing LCARS colors rather than an LCARS interface displaying web data.

missing_widgets.name: Paired Data Cells
missing_widgets.lcars_source: Balsavor (719/06, 1964/blank pattern). Label pill + value pill side by side.
missing_widgets.mesh_mapping: All key metrics: [CONTEXT] [42%], [TRIGGERS] [19], [SCHEMA] [v32], [BUDGET] [47/50]. Every fact occupies exactly two capsules.
missing_widgets.priority: HIGH — the most fundamental LCARS information unit. Currently our metrics display as HTML text, not as pill pairs.

missing_widgets.name: Sensor Bar Readouts (TNG hatched style)
missing_widgets.lcars_source: Home automation temp display — horizontal fill bars with fine vertical tick marks (hatched segmentation).
missing_widgets.mesh_mapping: Context pressure, autonomy budget, cognitive load, allostatic load. Current bars use plain CSS fills — TNG style adds characteristic vertical tick marks inside the bar.
missing_widgets.priority: MEDIUM — visual refinement of existing bars, not new functionality.

missing_widgets.name: Subsystem Status Blocks
missing_widgets.lcars_source: Home automation 'WEATHER SENSORS SUBSYSTEM' — bordered inset panel with label bar on top.
missing_widgets.mesh_mapping: Per-agent status blocks on Pulse station. Each agent gets a bordered inset: health indicator, budget remaining, current mode (generative/evaluative/neutral), last activity timestamp, photonic state.
missing_widgets.priority: MEDIUM — improves information hierarchy on Pulse station.
missing_widgets.mockup:
┌─ PSYCHOLOGY-AGENT ──────────────┐
  │ STATUS: ONLINE    MODE: NEUTRAL │
  │ BUDGET: 47/50     LAST: 2m ago  │
  │ PHOTONIC: evaluative ctx=0.35   │
  └─────────────────────────────────┘

missing_widgets.name: Multi-Column Alphanumeric Matrix
missing_widgets.lcars_source: Top-left dense number grid in Classic LCARS and themes-2025. Pure data density — organized numbers without charts.
missing_widgets.mesh_mapping: State.db table summary (28 tables, row counts, last-updated). Also: trigger fire_count matrix (T1-T20 in a grid with catch rates).
missing_widgets.priority: LOW — data-dense view for operators who want raw numbers.

missing_widgets.name: Section Divider Bars
missing_widgets.lcars_source: Balsavor — thin colored horizontal rules between logical zones within a screen.
missing_widgets.mesh_mapping: Separating metric groups within each station. Currently stations use whitespace only — divider bars add the LCARS visual rhythm.

missing_widgets.name: Stardate Display
missing_widgets.mesh_mapping: Mesh operational timestamp in header. Conversion formula: stardate = 100 * (year - 2323) + day_of_year * 100/365. Fun but functional — provides a single temporal reference.
missing_widgets.priority: LOW — cosmetic, but reinforces the design language.

missing_widgets.name: Rounded-Corner Inset Panel
missing_widgets.lcars_source: Holodeck programming — content display zones with rounded-corner borders (distinct from pill buttons).
missing_widgets.mesh_mapping: Deliberation log display, event feed, prediction calibration chart container. Provides the LCARS 'viewport' feel for scrollable content areas.
missing_widgets.priority: MEDIUM — improves visual containment of data-dense areas.

implementation_notes.css_approach: W1, W2, W3, W6, W7, W8 require CSS components only — no backend changes. W4 requires per-agent data from /api/status (already available). W5 requires /api/state-summary endpoint (new, trivial).
implementation_notes.component_library: Consider creating a shared LCARS CSS component library (lcars-components.css) that all agent dashboards import. Ensures visual consistency across the mesh.
implementation_notes.reference_images: Available at ~/Projects/ai-llm/lcars/ — balsavor, themes-2025, holodeck, home-automation.

body.recommendation: Start with W1 (numeric data grid) and W2 (paired data cells) — these represent the core LCARS visual vocabulary that distinguishes the dashboard from a generic web app. W3-W8 refine progressively. Total effort estimate: S for W1+W2 (CSS + JS), XS each for W3-W8.

claims.text: The Classic LCARS 47988 style guide palette matches our docs/lcars-style-guide.md color system — no palette corrections needed
claims.confidence_basis: Visual comparison of reference images against documented hex values

claims.text: W1 (numeric data grid) and W2 (paired data cells) represent the two most distinctive LCARS components absent from the current dashboard
claims.confidence_basis: Cross-reference of 4 LCARS reference images against dashboard-ux-redesign.md widget inventory

epistemic_flags: Visual comparison against reference images carries subjective judgment — another reviewer may identify different priority ordering
Stardate conversion formula varies across Trek canon — the one provided follows a common fan convention, not an official standard
